title: Label 309 encryption recipient slot
description: A single per-recipient slot in a sealed-PoE envelope, carrying the content-encryption key wrapped to one recipient. A slot is one of two KEM-discriminated CLOSED shapes — mirroring the grammar's `slot = classical-slot / hybrid-slot` — and the envelope-level `enc.kem` selects which: classical x25519 = `{ epk: bstr(32), wrap: bstr(48) }` (per-slot ephemeral X25519 public key + wrapped CEK); hybrid mlkem768x25519 = `{ kem_ct: bstr(1120), wrap: bstr(48) }` (the single 1120-byte X-Wing encapsulation — there is NO per-slot epk: the X25519 ephemeral is the trailing 32 bytes of `kem_ct`). `wrap` is the KEM-independent 48-byte wrapped CEK (32-byte CEK + 16-byte AEAD tag) in both shapes. This schema admits either shape structurally; binding the chosen shape to `enc.kem` (ENC_SLOT_INVALID_SHAPE) and the no-mixing rule (every slot in one envelope shares one `enc.kem`) are domain-pass checks. CBOR byte strings are modelled as lowercase-hex JSON strings.

classical-slot.title: Classical (x25519) slot
classical-slot.description: The per-slot X25519 ephemeral public key plus the wrapped CEK. Selected by `enc.kem` = x25519.

epk.$comment: 32-byte ephemeral X25519 public key; a wrong length emits KEM_EPK_LENGTH_MISMATCH.

hybrid-slot.title: Hybrid (mlkem768x25519) slot
hybrid-slot.description: The single 1120-byte X-Wing encapsulation plus the wrapped CEK. Selected by `enc.kem` = mlkem768x25519.
